description_long: This is a mid-rise office building with a rectangular footprint and a flat roof, featuring a modern glass curtain wall facade on its longer sides. The exterior is dominated by horizontal bands of dark-tinted windows separated by thin, light-colored spandrels, creating a regular, grid-like pattern. The shorter sides of the building are clad in a solid, light gray material with a narrow vertical strip of windows or vents running up the center. The roof is equipped with several mechanical units, vents, and antennae, indicating the presence of HVAC and communication systems. At the base of the building, there is a covered entryway supported by columns, and an adjacent parking lot is visible in some views. The overall design is functional and utilitarian, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century commercial architecture.
description: A rectangular, mid-rise office building with a glass curtain wall facade, flat roof, and regular horizontal window bands. The structure features solid end walls, rooftop mechanical units, and a covered entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main glass facade with horizontal window bands and a covered entryway supported by columns.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid gray wall with a narrow vertical strip of windows or vents and rooftop mechanical equipment.
description_view_2: Rear view with a similar glass facade as the front, adjacent to a large parking lot and visible rooftop structures.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid wall with vertical window strip and the parking lot extending from the building.
attribute_window_pattern: Regular horizontal bands of rectangular windows separated by thin spandrels, forming a continuous grid across the facade.
